rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOSEPH ALVARADO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Of Relief From Provision Of Government Code 945.5, Filed April 12, 2019
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER JOSEPH ALVARADO's Petition For Order Of Relief From Provision Of Government Code 945.5, Filed April 12, 2019. Petitioner Alvarado's petition for relief from the claims presentation requirement is denied. First, the crux of plaintiff's claim relates to a fire that occurred in 2006. The court lacks the power to grant relief because plaintiff's application to present a late claim was presented to respondent more than a year after the incident. (See Government Code § 946.6(c); 911.4(b).) Second, the petition does not articulate a clear basis for relief. To obtain relief, petitioner must show that the failure to present the claim was through mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. (See Santee v. Santa Clara County Office of Education (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 702, 708; Gov't Code § 946.6(c).) The petition fails to address this issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: QUAN DO VS. THOMAS B GARBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Otis Elevator Company's motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. When determining whether a settlement was entered into in good faith the court takes into account a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and defendant's proportionate liability, the amount paid in settlement, and recognizes that a settlor should pay less in settlement than if he were found liable after trial. The court also considers the financial condition of the settlor, the existence of insurance, and any evidence of collusion. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) In this case, the parties have not approximated plaintiff's total recovery and his statement of damages indicates that his damages are unsettled. (Chiu Decl., Ex. L.) Nevertheless, substantial evidence supports a finding that the settlement is in good faith. (See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n.6.) Otis presents evidence that its elevator was properly maintained and free from defect. (Chiu Dec., Ex. H [Hollinger Decl.] Since this evidence indicates that Otis has little if any potential liability, the settlement is in good faith regardless of the amount of plaintiff's claimed damages. (See Cahill v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 939, 962-966 [settlement amount of $25,000 was within ballpark of good faith settlements under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, despite plaintiff's multi-million dollar damages claim, when plaintiff had little if any chance of recovery at trial].) The objecting party (Garber) fails to meet its burden of showing, based on the information available at the time of settlement, that the settlement lacks good faith. (See Code Civ. Proc., §877.6(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Otis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: QUAN DO VS. THOMAS B GARBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Settlement Agreement Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY's Motion To File Settlement Agreement Under Seal. Defendant Otis Elevator Company's motion to seal is denied. Assuming there is an overriding interest in enforcing the parties' contract and encouraging settlement, Otis has not shown a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed. Otis's unsupported speculation that the disclosure of the settlement could mislead the public into believing that it is admitting liability or could result in disparagement of Otis's name is insufficient to meet its burden, particularly given the modest amount of the settlement. (See Huffy Corp. v. Superior Court (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 97, 105-107 [reversing trial court's order sealing documents including settlement agreement which had a confidentiality provision; "before papers can be sealed, there must be substantial prejudice to an overriding interest if the documents are made available to the public"]. So far as the record discloses, "[n]o prejudice to defendant's legitimate business and propriet[ar]y interests will occur if the settlement agreement is ordered unsealed." (Id. at 107.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant Garber is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARITA LOPEZ DE BOXERBAUM VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reclassification Of Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF MARGARITA LOPEZ DE BOXERBAUM's Motion For Reclassification Of Action. Plaintiff's motion to reclassify this action from a limited civil action to an unlimited civil action is granted. The motion must be granted if two conditions are satisfied: (1) the case is incorrectly classified and (b) the moving party shows good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier. (Code Civ. Proc., § 403.040, subd. (b).) Plaintiff meets both conditions. The trial court is required to determine whether, based on the record before the court, a judgment in excess of $25,000 is obtainable. (Ytuarte v. Superior Court (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 266, 279.) Plaintiff is not required to show with a "high level of certainty" that the damage award will exceed $25,000 to obtain reclassification. (Id.) Instead, plaintiff must present evidence to demonstrate a "possibility" the damages will exceed $25,000. (Id.) Here, this case is incorrectly classified as a "limited" matter because the Medicare lien is $70,765.09, which easily meets the standard. Plaintiff demonstrates good cause for not seeking reclassification earlier as Plaintiff did not receive or know about the $70,765.09 Medicare lien until May 20, 2019. (Chan Decl. Ex. C.) When Plaintiff filed the complaint on June 20, 2018, counsel knew Plaintiff's medical bills were being paid by either Medi-Cal or Medicare and filed the matter as a limited civil action believing that Medi-Cal/Medicare would be reimbursing medical providers at a much lower rate. (Chan Decl. 5.) Until the receipt of the Medicare lien, Plaintiff had no reason to amend the complaint to reclassify or file a motion to reclassify this matter from limited to unlimited. (Chan Decl. 20.) The City will not be prejudiced by the reclassification, as the complaint was not served until January 11, 2019, and Plaintiff's deposition has not yet been taken. Plaintiff shall pay the reclassification fee within 10 days of entry of the court's order. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 403.040 subd. (c)(1).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA VS. SAN FRAANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Margaret S. Flynn [Ccp 2030.300
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday July 31, 2019 line 3 - PLAINTIFFS GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA, GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA and ANDRES PANIAGUA's Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as follows: Not later than August 14, 2019 Defendant San Francisco Health Care and Rehab. Inc. shall provide amended responses to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories 29-34. The responses will be treated as confidential, used only for purposes of the instant litigation, not disclosed to any third parties except as necessary in connection with the litigation (e.g. to retained consultants or expert witnesses, court reporters, etc.), treated in accordance with Rules of Court 2.550 & 2.551, and filed under seal if submitted in connection with any future discovery motion. The scope of discovery is broad. It is not plaintiff's burden to show that its interrogatories seek "relevant, discoverable information"; rather, it is defendant's burden to justify its "objection[s] and failure to respond." Williams v. Sup Ct, 3 Cal. 5th 531, 541 (2017). On the limited record on this motion, it is not at all clear that the information sought is relevant or would be admissible for any purpose, but that is a determination that can be better made with full information available. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA VS. SAN FRAANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant San Francisco Health Care And Rehab Inc.'S Motion To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2019, Line 11, Defendant San Francisco Health Care And Rehab Inc.'s Motion To File Cross-Complaint. Defendants San Francisco Health Care Rehab and Rehab, Inc. and Marlen Arguedas's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is denied. Although leave to file a cross-complaint generally will be liberally granted, Defendants have unduly delayed in seeking leave to add a new party shortly before trial, as the record shows that they were aware in December 2017 of the alleged manufacturing or design defect, yet did not file the application for leave to file a cross-complaint until July 19, 2019. Plaintiff will be prejudiced if, as appears almost inevitable, the addition of a new party will require additional discovery and a continuance of the trial date, which was set on January 30, 2019. Further, if Defendants suffer an adverse verdict, they can bring a separate action for indemnity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SEBASTIEN SIKORSKI VS. ELIZABETH PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered; Points And Authorities; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF SEBASTIEN SIKORSKI'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Plaintiff and cross-defendant Sikorski's motion to vacate the default entered on the cross-complaint on June 13, 2019 is granted. There is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Plaintiff filed this motion within a reasonable time and he demonstrates excusable neglect. Plaintiff is representing himself, attempted to file the answer, and was not aware that the answer needed to be filed as a separate document. Plaintiff provides a proposed answer. Lastly, cross-complainant fails to demonstrate prejudice if the court grants the requested relief. Cross-defendant Sikorski shall file the proposed answer by August 9, 2019. rulings.Case Title: JAMES GUTIERREZ VS. OPORTUN FINANCIAL CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Plaintiffs Law Firms
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday July 31, 2019 line 4 - PLAINTIFF's JAMES GUTIERREZ's Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Plaintiffs Law Firms. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoenas to plaintiff's attorneys is granted. Plaintiff has not waived privilege by suing under his indemnity contract to recover the legal expenses he incurred. Defendant has not demonstrated that disclosure of privileged information is necessary to determine "which [legal] expenses [incurred by plaintiff] were incurred for which purpose," nor is it clear, as defendant argues (Opposition at 13:11-18), that to establish his claims plaintiff will require testimony from his attorneys regarding privileged matters, or that defendant needs access to privileged information to effectively cross-exam plaintiff's attorneys. Defendant's arguments lack substantial justification; pursuant to CCP § 1987.2 plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorneys fees of $9,870 (1 hr @ $670 + 16 hrs @ $575). This order is without prejudice to the issuance of new subpoenas that do not seek privileged information (e.g. subpoenas for redacted billing records). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
